title: Dashboard route: load and mutate todos via HTTP API
description: For the Dashboard screen (Stitch screen id 11611814ea7a4a2881d76c0c95fd3066), replace fixture/mock or direct SQLite-backed assumptions for todo lists with fetches to the HTTP JSON API from task 20-backend-http-json-api-todos-activity-feed. Preserve Stitch layout and tokens from tasks 13; add user-visible loading, empty, and error/retry states for API failures per architecture-brief error UX hints.
acceptance: Dashboard route loads todo list from the HTTP API (client or server fetch) using documented base URL strategy consistent with Next.js App Router setup.

Todo mutations on Dashboard (create, toggle, edit, delete as applicable to MVP) persist through the HTTP API or documented Server Action that delegates to the same use cases—not better-sqlite3 or local-only mocks on the default path.
No regression to Stitch structure: layout still matches refined ui_spec and design tokens for this screen.
Failed load/save surfaces readable error UI with recovery or retry where applicable.
Unit tests for pure presentation helpers (if any) remain in scope; no Prisma in client bundles.
implementation_notes: E2E: NewTodoForm uses id=dashboard-new-todo and aria-label='New todo' on the composer. STITCH_BASELINE_UI renders full-page DashboardStitchBaseline (§4.2); pixelmatch may still differ from PNG due to font rasterization and remote image bytes.
